title: skopeo copy --multi-arch with specific platforms fails with "blob unknown to registry" when manifest list contains other platforms
body:
Description

When using skopeo copy with --multi-arch "linux/amd64,linux/arm64" to copy only specific platforms from a multi-arch image, the command fails with blob unknown to registry error. The issue appears to be that the manifest list still references other platforms (e.g., linux/386, linux/arm/v7) that were not uploaded to the destination registry.

Steps to Reproduce

Source image is a multi-arch manifest list containing multiple platforms:

bash
skopeo inspect docker://registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9 --raw | jq '.manifests[].platform'
Output shows platforms: linux/amd64, linux/arm64, linux/386, linux/arm/v7, etc.

Attempt to copy only linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 platforms:

bash
skopeo copy \
--multi-arch "linux/amd64,linux/arm64" \
--src-tls-verify=false \
--dest-tls-verify=false \
docker://registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9 \
docker://myregistry.com/registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9
Error occurs:

text
FATA[0001] Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats:
application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(uploading manifest 3.9 to myregistry.com/registry.k8s.io/pause: blob unknown to registry),
application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(uploading manifest 3.9 to myregistry.com/registry.k8s.io/pause: blob unknown to registry)
Expected Behavior

When specifying --multi-arch "linux/amd64,linux/arm64", skopeo should:

Download only the specified platform images

Create a new manifest list containing ONLY these platforms

Successfully push the filtered manifest list to the destination registry

Actual Behavior

skopeo attempts to push the original manifest list (containing all platforms) to the destination registry, but the blobs for unspecified platforms (e.g., linux/386, linux/arm/v7) don't exist in the destination, causing blob unknown errors.

Root Cause Analysis

Based on code inspection (parseMultiArch function in cmd/skopeo/copy.go), when --multi-arch "linux/amd64,linux/arm64" is provided:

go
case "linux/amd64,linux/arm64":
return copy.CopySpecificImages, platforms, nil
The function correctly returns CopySpecificImages with platform filters. However, when building the manifest list for upload, skopeo appears to preserve the original manifest list structure instead of creating a filtered one that excludes unspecified platforms.


skopeo version: 1.23.0

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yes. We also need to expose `StripSparseManifestList`. That is coming in the future. Cc: @aguidirh

Thanks for your report @viccch,

The feature to keep the manifest list only with the archs requested is going to be implemented in PR #2892 so please keep watching this one.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
